about.description: A fast mobile client for intervals.icu with a focus on maps and visual analytics.
about.disclaimer: Veloq is an independent, open-source project. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to intervals.icu in any way.
about.garminNote: Activity data may include information from Garmin, Strava, Polar, Wahoo, and other devices connected to your intervals.icu account.
about.mapAttribution: Map tiles provided by OpenStreetMap, Carto, and Stadia Maps. Map data copyright OpenStreetMap contributors.
about.title: About & Legal

activity.chartHint: Hold to scrub • Hold chip for axis

explanations.conditions: Temperature from weather data or your device sensor.
explanations.energy: Estimated energy expenditure from heart rate, power, and duration.
explanations.heartRate: Average heart rate during the activity.
explanations.trainingLoad: Training Stress Score (TSS) quantifies training load based on duration and intensity relative to your threshold.
explanations.yourForm: Your Form (TSB) on this day. This is a daily value based on your overall training, not specific to this activity. TSB = Fitness (CTL) minus Fatigue (ATL).

activityDetail.noMatchedSectionsDescription: Sections matching this activity will appear here
activityDetail.noRouteMatchDescription: Complete this route a few more times to start tracking your performance, or expand your timeline in Settings to include older activities.
activityDetail.bodyShapeRandom: Body chosen at random — no gender in intervals.icu profile

alerts.cacheCorruptionMessage: Local data cache was corrupted and has been cleared. Your data will be refreshed from the server.
alerts.clearCacheMessage: This will clear all cached activity data, activity tracks, and route matching data, then start reloading. This may take a few minutes.
alerts.clearRouteCacheMessage: This will clear all route matching data and immediately re-analyze activity tracks.
alerts.disconnectMessage: This will remove your API credentials from the app. You will need to enter them again to use the app.
alerts.failedToClear: Failed to clear cache. Please try again.
alerts.failedToDisconnect: Failed to disconnect. Please try again.
alerts.syncAllMessage: This will sync up to 10 years of activity data in the background. This enables deeper route analysis and comparison.

fitnessScreen.fatigueDescription: is a 7-day exponentially weighted moving average of your training load.
fitnessScreen.fitnessDescription: is a 42-day exponentially weighted moving average of your training load.
fitnessScreen.formDescription: is fitness minus fatigue. Train in the
fitnessScreen.title: Fitness & Form

guidance.highRisk: Accumulated fatigue is very high. Consider rest or easy sessions until form rises above -30.
guidance.optimal: Productive training range. Your body is absorbing training stress effectively.
guidance.greyZone: Moderate training load. Neither rested nor heavily fatigued — maintenance territory.
guidance.fresh: Well-rested. Good timing for a race or high-intensity test. Fitness may decline if sustained.
guidance.transition: Very rested but losing fitness stimulus. Consider resuming structured training.

licenses.footer: Tap any license badge to view the full license text. Tap the link icon to visit the project repository.
licenses.intro: Veloq is built with open-source software. This screen lists the third-party libraries and their licenses.
licenses.title: Third-Party Licenses

insights.title: Insights
insights.noInsights: Insights will appear as you train
insights.noInsightsHint: Complete more activities to see personalized insights
insights.loadBody: TSS: {{currentTss}} this week vs {{previousTss}} last week. Duration: {{currentDuration}} vs {{previousDuration}}.
insights.volumeBody: Training duration: {{current}} this week vs {{previous}} last week. This measures time, not training load (TSS).

hrvTrend.trendingUpBody: Your 7-day HRV average ({{avg}} ms) is rising based on {{days}} days of data.
hrvTrend.trendingDownBody: Your 7-day HRV average ({{avg}} ms) is declining based on {{days}} days of data.
hrvTrend.stableBody: Your 7-day HRV average ({{avg}} ms) is within normal range based on {{days}} days of data.

weeklyLoad.title: Training load {{percent}}% {{direction}} average
weeklyLoad.body: This week: {{acute}} TSS vs 4-week average of {{chronic}} TSS ({{percent}}% difference).

stalePr.title: {{section}}: PR might be beatable
stalePr.subtitle: PR at {{prFtp}}W FTP, now {{currentFtp}}W (+{{gainPercent}}%)
stalePr.body: {{section}}: PR of {{prTime}} set at {{prFtp}}W FTP. Current FTP: {{currentFtp}}W.
stalePr.methodology: Compares FTP at PR time vs current FTP. Shows sections where fitness metrics have changed since the PR was set.

restDay.tomorrowPatternBody: Based on {{count}} previous activities for this day of the week.

methodology.banister: Calculates Training Stress Balance from the difference between fitness (CTL) and fatigue (ATL).
methodology.hrv: Compares your heart rate variability trend against your 7-day baseline to assess recovery status.
methodology.kmeans: Groups your activities by day, duration, and sport type to identify recurring training patterns.
methodology.prDetection: Compares your latest section time against all previous efforts to detect personal records.
methodology.sectionTrend: Tracks median performance on frequently visited sections over time.
methodology.periodComparison: Compares training metrics between consecutive weeks to track progression.

insights.disclaimer: Training metrics are estimates based on published exercise science. Individual responses vary. Not medical or coaching advice.
insights.aboutInsightsBody: Training metrics are estimates based on published exercise science models. Individual responses vary significantly. These insights are informational only — not medical or coaching advice.
insights.aboutBody: Training metrics are estimates based on published exercise science models. Individual responses vary significantly. These insights are informational only — not medical or coaching advice.

efficiencyTrend.title: HR trending down on {{name}}
efficiencyTrend.subtitle: {{hrChange}} bpm lower over {{efforts}} efforts
efficiencyTrend.body: Over {{efforts}} efforts on {{name}}, your heart rate at the same pace has dropped by {{hrChange}} bpm.

strengthBalance.noRecentVolume: No volume in the last 4 weeks — only appearing in the wider selected period.
strengthBalance.volumeAppeared: Volume appeared in the last two weeks after a quieter start.
strengthBalance.volumeUp: Recent 2-week average is {{percent}}% above the prior 2 weeks.
strengthBalance.volumeDown: Recent 2-week average is {{percent}}% below the prior 2 weeks.
strengthBalance.volumeSteady: Recent 2-week average is holding steady versus the earlier part of the month.

settings.defaultMapHint: 'Default' uses the map style set above
settings.disconnectDescription: This will remove your API credentials from the app. You will need to enter them again to use the app.

settings.primarySportHint: Affects home screen metrics and default stats page view.
settings.primarySportHintCycling: FTP shown in feed header. Performance page shows Power Curve, power zones, eFTP trend, and decoupling.
settings.primarySportHintRunning: Threshold pace and LTHR shown in feed header. Performance page shows Pace Curve, running power, and HR zones.
settings.primarySportHintSwimming: CSS (Critical Swim Speed) shown in feed header. Performance page shows Swim Pace Curve and HR zones.
settings.routeMatchingDescription: Automatically detect repeated routes from your activities
settings.syncAllHistoryDescription: This will sync up to 10 years of activity data in the background. This enables deeper route analysis and comparison.
settings.syncInProgress: Please wait for the current sync to complete.
settings.terrain3DHint: Smart mode shows 3D for hilly and mountainous terrain
settings.title: Settings

settings.unitsImperialHint: Distances in miles, elevation in feet, temperature in Fahrenheit
settings.unitsMetricHint: Distances in kilometers, elevation in meters, temperature in Celsius

settings.cacheModeAmbientHint: Tiles cached as you browse maps
settings.cacheModeStandardHint: Slowly downloads tiles for your activities
settings.cacheModeMaximumHint: Slowly downloads 5 km radius · all styles
settings.notEnoughStorage: Not enough storage for Maximum mode

stats.addEventsHint: Add events on intervals.icu to see them here
stats.completeActivitiesHeatmap: Complete activities to see your calendar heatmap
stats.completeActivitiesHr: Complete activities with heart rate data to see zone distribution
stats.completeActivitiesPower: Complete activities with power meter data to see zone distribution
stats.completeActivitiesYearComparison: Complete activities to see year-over-year comparison
stats.completeDecouplingHint: Complete a sustained effort with power and heart rate data to see decoupling analysis
stats.completePowerActivities: Complete power-based activities to see your FTP trend
stats.createWorkoutsHint: Create workouts on intervals.icu to see them here
stats.decouplingExplanation: Decoupling measures cardiac drift - how much your heart rate rises relative to power output during sustained efforts. Lower is better, indicating efficient aerobic energy production.

statsScreen.garminNote: Charts may include data from Garmin devices
statsScreen.paceCurveInfo: CS (critical speed) and D' are calculated using the 2 parameter model. Intervals.icu uses your best 1k, 2k, 3k, 4k and 5k times keeping those that take between 2 and 15+ minutes.
statsScreen.powerCurveInfo: Estimated FTP is calculated using power curves from FastFitness.Tips and Morton's 3 parameter critical power model. The algorithm requires just 1 max effort of between 180 seconds and 30 minutes. W' is relative to eFTP.
statsScreen.title: Performance

wellness.connectHint: Connect a wearable or log wellness data in Intervals.icu
wellness.smoothingDescription: Apply a rolling average to smooth out daily variations and show trends more clearly.
wellness.smoothingHint: Auto selects the best smoothing for the time range
wellness.trendHint: HRV, sleep, and resting HR trends will appear here when data is logged

activityRecorded.title: Activity Recorded
activityRecorded.body: New activity synced. Open the app for details.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.brief: Your athlete ID and a push token will be stored on our server (90-day expiry). Notifications are routed through Apple or Google but all insight content is generated on your device. No personal data is stored. You can disable at any time.
privacy.body: When you enable notifications, a device token and your athlete ID are stored on auth.veloq.fit (our server) with automatic 90-day expiry. When a new activity is detected, a minimal push message (containing only an activity ID) is routed through Apple's or Google's push notification service to reach your device. The actual notification content (PR details, insights) is generated entirely on your device — Apple and Google never see it. No activity data, GPS, or personal information is stored on our server. You can disable notifications at any time, which immediately removes your data from our server.

settings.title: Notifications
settings.requiresOAuth: To enable notifications, disconnect and reconnect using the OAuth login flow instead of a personal API key.
settings.privacyHint: Stores a push token and athlete ID on our server (90-day expiry). See veloq.fit/privacy
settings.stravaNote: Note: Strava-sourced activities do not trigger notifications.

v030.notificationsBody: Get notified when you set a personal record or hit a fitness milestone. All insights are generated entirely on your device.
v030.requiresOAuth: To enable notifications, disconnect and reconnect using the OAuth login flow instead of a personal API key. Your app works fully without them.

v022.mapStylesBody: Choose light, dark, or satellite map styles with smart 3D terrain for hilly activities.
v022.mapStylesHint: High-res satellite covers Switzerland and France. Smart 3D activates for activities with 30 m+ gain and 3 m/km+ gradient.
v022.heatmapBody: Your training calendar on the Health page. Tap any day to see your wellness data — HRV, resting heart rate, and sleep — sync across charts in real time.
v022.heatmapTip: Press and hold on the health metrics to see the values. The activity heatmap updates to match.
v022.fitnessBody: Track your fitness, fatigue, and form with color-coded zones. Know when you're fresh and ready to perform.
